focus: Expose live Cranfield evaluation metrics and representative correct, wrong, and missed examples in the public UI and API.
reason_started: The operator asked where evaluation results are and noted that people should see some correct and wrong examples.
reason_ended: Evaluation results are deployed in the UI, exposed as JSON, and verified through local validation and two public checks.

validators_executed.name: npm run validate
validators_executed.evidence: 17 tests passed, including evaluation endpoint and UI content assertions.

validators_executed.name: public root evaluation UI checks
validators_executed.evidence: Root UI includes Evaluation Results and representative examples.

validators_executed.name: public evaluation endpoint checks
validators_executed.evidence: Evaluation endpoints expose aggregate metrics and strong/mixed/weak examples.

github_submission.blocker: Evaluation UI and evidence changes remain in the working tree for review; no explicit commit or push request was made.

reusable_lessons: Public evaluation pages should include concrete correct, weak, and missed examples in addition to aggregate metrics.
downstream_impacts: Future BEIR and ESCI phases should expose comparable evaluation examples before public release.
content_seeds: Phase 1 article can use the strong/mixed/weak examples to explain BM25 baseline behavior.
evidence_quality_notes: Examples are grounded in the live Cranfield evaluation artifact and live OpenSearch result checks.
Result-level examples are curated rather than full 225-query public dump to keep the page readable.

supports_mission: Makes evaluation evidence transparent to readers.
Shows baseline wins and failures without pretending the v0 search is complete.
missing: Phase 1 article linked to commit and endpoint.
goal_drift_review.mission_alignment: GEN-007 strengthens Phase 1 evidence without changing the baseline retrieval architecture.
